description: Tracks tweet IDs and accounts already surfaced in daily sessions. Never repeat these.

surfaced_posts.reply_text:
there's a type 3 - deep in claude code, no paying customer anywhere

the tool doesn't pick the problem
point it at something real and yeah, it runs while you sleep

replied_posts.reply_text:
the million isn't the hard part

most founders are still figuring out distribution - once that clicks, it's the same loop on repeat
the first 10 customers teach you how to sell. that's it. that's the whole thing.

agents are great for daily execution - no template, just get it done

in prod it's different. when the same error hits 10 users in a row, you need a dev to trace exactly where it broke
"control" is just the polite word for that

staying above the api line is the right call

voice is still rented though - gemini today, probably something else in 3 months
the dependency just moves lower

most founders put their friendliest customers in these chats, not the demanding ones - that's where the useful feedback dies

engineering side is the same
your most demanding users need your sharpest engineers in there, not whoever's free to face the music

there's a type 3 - deep in claude code, no paying customer anywhere

the tool doesn't pick the problem
point it at something real and yeah, it runs while you sleep

skipped_accounts.reason: wrong person — photographer, not the bootstrapped founder
